metadata.description: Matt's portable, ADS-agnostic dotfiles plugin marketplace — the public/air-gapped counterparts of the matt-plugins (Azure-DevOps) marketplace. Designed to run on a Sonnet-4.5 / 200k orchestrator.

plugins.name: orchestrator
plugins.description: Context-frugal, beads-backed multi-agent orchestrator for a constrained (Sonnet-4.5 / 200k) context window. A high-altitude session loops over ready beads and dispatches disposable worker subagents (scout/planner/implementer/verifier/reviewer/investigator/test-writer), keeping its window near-empty. Ships the orchestration-protocol + plan-first-dispatch + metrics-reporting skills and the commands /implement, /triage, /cover, /notify (file an interjection bead), and /gather-metrics (structured run metrics). Plan-first-dispatch makes the orchestrator plan before implementing and auto-approve the plan bounded by its charter. The ADS-agnostic counterpart of brain-orchestrator.

plugins.name: appium-windows-uia-testing
plugins.description: Appium UI-automation testing for Windows desktop apps (WPF/WinUI/Win32) over UI Automation. Two skills (appium-windows-uia-setup to bootstrap the test project + Appium/driver + CI; appium-windows-uia-testing for AccessibilityId-over-XPath, no-UIA-peer placement traps, per-fixture wall-clock-kill runner, debug-log triage, screenshot helper), a runnable example/ app, and a self-contained beads-backed orchestration layer — the /build-component-tests command + scout/reviewer/component-test-builder (parallel authoring) and appium-interactive-runner (single serialized live-app executor + debugger) agents — sized for a Sonnet-4.5 / 200k orchestrator.

plugins.name: dev-workflow
plugins.description: A trimmed, language-agnostic software-development lifecycle for constrained (Sonnet-4.5 / 200k) workflows — the portable counterpart of the full superpowers plugin. The chain brainstorming -> writing-plans -> executing-plans | subagent-driven-development (tiered-subagent-dispatch + verifying-subagent-output) -> review gates -> finishing-a-development-branch, plus using-git-worktrees. A SessionStart hook injects a thin bootstrap; commands /brainstorm /plan /execute /finish. Pairs with the orchestrator plugin for the worker subagents.

plugins.name: dotnet-style
plugins.description: Default stack and workflow opinions for modern .NET (C# 12+, dotnet 8+): preferred libraries (NUnit 4, CommunityToolkit.Mvvm, Serilog two-stage bootstrap), code conventions (sealed records + required init, Nullable + ImplicitUsings), the deterministic ReSharper -> dotnet format -> XAML Styler toolchain, and the csharp-ls LSP reality. Ships copy-able assets (jb + xstyler tool manifest, just style recipes, changed-files script). The .NET-specific companion to dev-workflow.

plugins.name: plugin-self-improvement
plugins.description: Continuous self-improvement for the Claude Code plugin collection. Turns 'aha' moments — realizations, contradictions-with-reality, 'this skill should have told me X' — into tracked work before they evaporate: the capture-plugin-insight skill triggers on a meta-insight about a plugin/skill/command/agent and the /improve-plugin command captures it explicitly, both filing a bead labeled `plugin-improvement` under a long-lived self-improvement epic bead (discovered by the `plugin-self-improvement` label). A thin SessionStart hook reminds the model the capability exists. The portable, beads-backed counterpart of the ADS plugin-self-improvement plugin in the matt-plugins marketplace.
